model_weaknesses: Brevity bias: Short reviews containing exclamation marks and strong sentiments are sometimes misclassified as highly helpful.
Sentiment skew: The model tends to label reviews with mixed constructive feedback (combining positive and negative aspects) with lower helpfulness values, despite their actual utility.
Generic spam: High frequency of certain generic adjectives ('great', 'best', 'perfect') triggers a false positive helpful classification even when the review lacks substantial descriptive features.

failures.reason: General positive sentiment and length biased the hybrid XGBoost, missing the lack of concrete detailed specifications in the review text.

failures.text: It worked for a week, then died. I contacted support and they replaced it free of charge, which was nice.
failures.reason: Negative sentiment keywords and structural complexity (longer phrases with punctuation) confused the model, ignoring useful real-world usage specifications.
